name: Japanese Braised Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Leaving stems on, cut eggplants in half lengthwise.
2. Mix together the sherry, soy sauce, and molasses in a bowl.
3. Oil a baking pan.
4. Pour the sherry mixture into the pan, place the eggplant slices face down, cover tightly, and bake at 350°F for 45 minutes.
5. Brown the cubed tempeh, half cup of onions, fennel seeds, and cayenne pepper in oil over medium heat for about 20 minutes, stirring frequently to avoid burning.
6. In a separate pot, sauté the remaining onion, coriander, remaining fennel until the onions are translucent.
7. Add the green bell pepper and mushrooms, and sauté for an additional 15 to 20 minutes.
8. Using a slotted spoon, lift the tempeh and onions from the oil and stir them into the sautéed vegetables.
9. Stir in the tomato paste and about 2 tablespoons of the braising liquid from the eggplant pan.
10. Add salt to taste to the filling.
11. Turn the eggplant halves over so that the mashed pulp is pushed to the sides, leaving a hollow center. Fill each hollow with about one-quarter of the filling.
12. Cover the pan tightly and bake at 350°F for an additional 20 minutes until piping hot.
13. Serve on a bed of rice, pour some juice from the baking pan over it, and sprinkle with chopped scallions and toasted sesame seeds.
